brown.songTitle: Jeanie with the Light Brown Hair
brown.linePrev: While her gentle fingers will cull them no more:
brown.line: Oh! I sigh for Jeanie with the light brown hair
brown.lineNext: Floating, like a vapor, on the soft summer air
stanza: I sigh for Jeanie, but her light form strayed
Far from the fond hearts round her native glade;
Her smiles have vanished and her sweet songs flown
Flitting like the dreams that have cheered us and gone
Now the nodding wild flowers may wither on the shore
While her gentle fingers will cull them no more:
Oh! I sigh for Jeanie with the light brown hair
Floating, like a vapor, on the soft summer air

bawl.lineNext: Sibyl sea-cow, all done up in a bow
stanza: Awful atoll —
O, incalculable indiscreetness and sorrow!
Bawl bellow:
Sibyl sea-cow, all done up in a bow
Toddle and roll;
Teethe an impalpable bit of leather
While yarrow, heather and hollyhock
Awkwardly molt along the shore

bellow.lineNext: Sibyl sea-cow, all done up in a bow
stanza: Awful atoll —
O, incalculable indiscreetness and sorrow!
Bawl bellow:
Sibyl sea-cow, all done up in a bow
Toddle and roll;
Teethe an impalpable bit of leather
While yarrow, heather and hollyhock
Awkwardly molt along the shore

partner.line: Teethe an impalpable bit of leather

blissful.line: Life is thundering blissful towards death
stanza: Life is thundering blissful towards death
Then down and down
And down and down
And down and deeper
Stoke, without sound
The blameless flames
You endless sleeper

barrenly.linePrev: Beneath the sheeting banks of air
barrenly.lineNext: When I could speak, it was too late
stanza: In this life, who did you love
Beneath the drifting ashes
Beneath the sheeting banks of air
That barrenly bore our rations?

barley.linePrev: Didn't you see my heart leap
barley.line: Like a pup in the constant barley?
barley.lineNext: In this new life, where did you crouch
stanza: When I could speak, it was too late
Didn't you hear me calling?
Didn't you see my heart leap
Like a pup in the constant barley?

brakeless.linePrev: While the stars skid away below
brakeless.lineNext: Falling silent as gavels in the snow
stanza: Hung from the underbelly of the earth
While the stars skid away below
Gormless and brakeless, gravel-loose
Falling silent as gavels in the snow

boatload.line: We came by the boatload and were immobilized
boatload.lineNext: Worshiping volcanoes, charting the loping skies
stanza: We came by the boatload and were immobilized
Worshiping volcanoes, charting the loping skies
The tides of the earth left us bound and calcified
And made as obstinate as obsidian, unmoving, save our eyes
Just mooning and blinking from faces marked with coal
Ash cooling and shrinking, cracks loud as thunder rolling
I swear I know you, you know me, where have we met before?
Tell me true, to whose authority do you consign your soul?

birdbath.linePrev: Soaking and felling everything in its path
birdbath.line: And welling in my heart like a birdbath
birdbath.lineNext: It is too short, the day we are born
stanza: I had a dream you came to me
Said, "You shall not do me harm anymore"
And with your knife, you evicted my life
From its little lighthouse on the seashore
And I saw that my blood had no bounds
Spreading in a circle like an atom bomb
Soaking and felling everything in its path
And welling in my heart like a birdbath

blackguard.linePrev: Heard the cup drop, thought, "Well, that's why they keep them around"
blackguard.line: The blackguard sat hard down with no head on him now
blackguard.lineNext: And I felt so bad, 'cause I didn't know how
stanza: From the courtyard, I floated in and watched it go down
Heard the cup drop, thought, "Well, that's why they keep them around"
The blackguard sat hard down with no head on him now
And I felt so bad, 'cause I didn't know how
To feel bad enough to make him proud

blight.linePrev: Shake her skirts and scatter there
blight.line: A shrieking, six-legged millionaire with a blight in his sockets
blight.lineNext: Miss Montez, the Countess of Lansfeld
stanza: Here's Lola — ta da! — to do her famous Spider Dance for you
Lighten up your pockets
Shake her skirts and scatter there
A shrieking, six-legged millionaire with a blight in his sockets

bavaria.linePrev: Miss Montez, the Countess of Lansfeld
bavaria.line: Appealed to the King of Bavaria
bavaria.lineNext: Saying, "Pretty papa, if you are my friend
stanza: Miss Montez, the Countess of Lansfeld
Appealed to the King of Bavaria
Saying, "Pretty papa, if you are my friend
Mister Daddy Longlegs, they are at it again
Can I see you?"

partner.line: Saying, "Pretty papa, if you are my friend

bouquet.linePrev: Ask and you will receive," it is said
bouquet.line: I threw my bouquet and I knocked 'em dead
bouquet.lineNext: Bottle of white, bottle of red
stanza: You asked my hand, hired a band
"In your heart is all that you need
Ask and you will receive," it is said
I threw my bouquet and I knocked 'em dead
Bottle of white, bottle of red
Helpless as a child when you held me in your arms
And I knew that no other could ever love me as you loved
Love me as you loved, but help me, I'm leaving

buoy.linePrev: Sitting up in the star-smoking air
buoy.lineNext: Don't you worry for me, will you have one on me!
stanza: It was dark, I was drunk and half-dead
And we slept, knocking heads
Sitting up in the star-smoking air
Knocking heads like buoys
Don't you worry for me, will you have one on me!

buoy.linePrev: If they find me racing white horses
buoy.line: They'll not take me for a buoy
buoy.lineNext: Let me be weak, let me sleep
stanza: If they find me racing white horses
They'll not take me for a buoy
Let me be weak, let me sleep
And dream of sheep
The sight of bridges and balloons
Makes calm canaries irritable;
They caw and claw all afternoon:
"Catenaries and dirigibles
Brace and buoy the living-room—
A loom of metal, warp—woof—wimble"
And a thimble's worth of milky moon
Can touch hearts larger than a thimble

bump.line: Like a bump on a bump on a log, baby;
bump.lineNext: Like I'm in a fistfight with the fog, baby;
stanza: And the tilt of this strange nation
And the will to remain for the duration
(waving the flag
Feeling it drag)
Like a bump on a bump on a log, baby;
Like I'm in a fistfight with the fog, baby;
Step, ball-change, and a pirouette!

bump.linePrev: Anger and no one can heal it
bump.line: Shoves a little bump into the momentum
bump.lineNext: It's just a little lump but you feel it
stanza: Moves like a fist through the traffic
Anger and no one can heal it
Shoves a little bump into the momentum
It's just a little lump but you feel it
In the creases and the shadows
With a rattling deep emotion
The cool, cool river
Sweeps the wild, white ocean

bump.linePrev: Ten feet of water at the bottom of the lake
bump.line: I imagined trout bumping against me in the low diminished light
bump.lineNext: Holding my breath trying to be a boulder
stanza: At night I sit and picture myself curled up beneath
Ten feet of water at the bottom of the lake
I imagined trout bumping against me in the low diminished light
Holding my breath trying to be a boulder
Eroding, to join you in re-mingling with a background
Of churned muck coalescing in the dark
But to get ground back down to matter only
Eternal and dumb becoming not a thing
Abdicating form

bulletproof.linePrev: Do you remember staring up at the stars
bulletproof.line: So far away in their bulletproof cars?
bulletproof.lineNext: When we heard the rushing, slow intake
stanza: Do you remember staring up at the stars
So far away in their bulletproof cars?
When we heard the rushing, slow intake
Of the dark, dark water and the engine breaks

blacksmith.linePrev: A little baby fussing over my legs
blacksmith.line: There is a blacksmith and there is a shepherd
stanza: We take a walk along the dirty lake
Hear the goose cussing at me over her eggs
You poor little cousin, I don't want your dregs
A little baby fussing over my legs
There is a blacksmith and there is a shepherd
And there is a butcher-boy
And there is a barber who's cutting and cutting away
At my only joy
I saw a rabbit as slick as a knife
And as pale as a candlestick
And I had thought it'd be harder to do
But I caught her, and skinned her quick
Held her there, kicking and mewling
Upended, unspooling, unsung and blue
Told her "Wherever you go, little runaway bunny, I will find you"
And then she ran, as they're liable to do

barber.lineNext: She had become a vocal martyr in the vegan press

She had become a vocal martyr in the vegan press

They were looking for a male with a bad hair cut
Enter tumbleweed, exit love
And our affaire d'amour was set on self-destruct

backslide.linePrev: The twinned and cast-off shells reveal a single heart of white
backslide.lineNext: Ancient border, sink past the West
stanza: And in an infinite backslide:
Ancient border, sink past the West
Like a sword at the bearer's fall
I can't claim that I knew you best
But did you know me at all?

bedraggle.linePrev: In spite of all the time that we spend on it:
bedraggle.line: On one bedraggled ghost of a sonnet!
bedraggle.lineNext: While outside, the wild boars root
stanza: And all at once it came to me
And I wrote and hunched 'till four-thirty
But that vestal light
It burns out with the night
In spite of all the time that we spend on it:
On one bedraggled ghost of a sonnet!
While outside, the wild boars root
Without bending a bough underfoot —
O it breaks my heart; I don't know how they do it
So don't ask me!

Without bending a bough underfoot —
O it breaks my heart; I don't know how they do it
So don't ask me!

brine.linePrev: So I reach for her hand and her eyes turn to poison
brine.line: And her hair turns to splinters and her flesh turns to brine
brine.lineNext: She leaps across the room, she stands in the window
stanza: So I reach for her hand and her eyes turn to poison
And her hair turns to splinters and her flesh turns to brine
She leaps across the room, she stands in the window
Screams that my first-born will surely be blind
Sing a-to Lorelei-oh

partner.line: Screams that my first-born will surely be blind

stanza: And the mealy worms
In the brine will burn
In a salty pyre
Among the fawns and ferns

bearing.linePrev: With four fine masts and lateen sails
stanza: We sailed away on a winter's day
With fate as malleable as clay
But ships are fallible, I say
And the nautical, like all things, fades
And I can recall our caravel:
A little wicker beetle shell
With four fine masts and lateen sails
Its bearings on Cair Paravel

brace.linePrev: So I guess I'll have to do it while I'm here
brace.line: And I won't breathe the bracing air when I'm gone
brace.lineNext: And I can't even worry 'bout my cares when I'm gone
stanza: And I won't breathe the bracing air when I'm gone
And I can't even worry 'bout my cares when I'm gone
Won't be asked to do my share when I'm gone

brace.linePrev: And I'll tie 'em to your shoulders and your feet
brace.line: I'll steal a hacksaw from my dad and cut the braces off your legs
brace.lineNext: And we'll bury them tonight out in the cornfield
stanza: Then we'll spit on Ronnie Arnold and flip him the bird
And slash the tires on the school bus, now don't say a word
I'll take a rusty nail and scratch your initials in my arm
And I'll show you how to sneak up on the roof of the drugstore
I'll take the spokes from your wheelchair and a magpie's wings
And I'll tie 'em to your shoulders and your feet
I'll steal a hacksaw from my dad and cut the braces off your legs
And we'll bury them tonight out in the cornfield
Just put a church key in your pocket, we'll hop that freight train in the hall
We'll slide all the way down the drain to New Orleans in the fall
The sight of bridges and balloons
Makes calm canaries irritable;
They caw and claw all afternoon:
"Catenaries and dirigibles
Brace and buoy the living-room—
A loom of metal, warp—woof—wimble"
And a thimble's worth of milky moon
Can touch hearts larger than a thimble

burnin.linePrev: He was headed down to Knoxville with the weekly load
burnin.line: You could smell the whiskey burnin' down Copperhead Road
stanza: Now, Daddy ran the whiskey in a big-block Dodge
Bought it at an auction at the Mason's Lodge
"Johnson County Sheriff" painted on the side
He just shot a coat of primer, then he looked inside
Well, him and my uncle tore that engine down
I still remember that rumblin' sound
And then the Sheriff came around in the middle of the night
Heard Mama cryin', knew somethin' wasn't right
He was headed down to Knoxville with the weekly load
You could smell the whiskey burnin' down Copperhead Road

burnin.linePrev: You see this train I'm riding
burnin.lineNext: And its wheels are bound to roll right by you
stanza: You see this train I'm riding
It's burnin' up the coal
And its wheels are bound to roll right by you
Honey, won't you jump my train?

burnin.linePrev: Without a penny or a worth to his name
burnin.line: Wasting his life on a burnin' desire
burnin.lineNext: And chasing that honky tonk flame
stanza: I was out of my mind the first night we met
I was feeling lonely and blue
I'd just moved to the country to get me some rest
The city can put a hurtin' on you
Especially a fella from eastern Kentucky
Without a penny or a worth to his name
Wasting his life on a burnin' desire
And chasing that honky tonk flame

burnin.line: Let's follow that fire truck, I think your house is burnin' down
burnin.lineNext: And go down to the hobo jungle and kill some rattlesnakes with a trowel
stanza: Let's follow that fire truck, I think your house is burnin' down
And go down to the hobo jungle and kill some rattlesnakes with a trowel
And we'll break all the windows in the old Anderson place
We'll steal a bunch of boysenberries and I'll smear 'em on your face
I'll get a dollar from my mama's purse and buy that skull and crossbones ring
And you can wear it round your neck on an old piece of string

burnin.linePrev: All I did was slow down and quit actin' that way
burnin.line: Burnin' my barn in this honky tonk flame
stanza: But I got me a woman with a love so true
Darlin' to me but that's misses to you
All I did was slow down and quit actin' that way
Burnin' my barn in this honky tonk flame

burnin.linePrev: You just got to slow down, quit actin' that way
burnin.line: Burnin' your barn in this honky tonk flame
burnin.lineNext: You were sweet to the touch
stanza: I'm a woman with a love so true
Truth of the matter I'd give it to you
You just got to slow down, quit actin' that way
Burnin' your barn in this honky tonk flame

brian.linePrev: The last of the family reserve
brian.line: And my friend Brian Temple, he thought he could make it
brian.lineNext: So from the third story, he jumped
stanza: And my friend Brian Temple, he thought he could make it
So from the third story, he jumped
And he missed the swimming pool only by inches
And everyone said he was drunk

In a Pendleton shirt, songs and gentle words
Granted wishes

Put all the stars to death

Just checkin' out to hide from life
And all of our problems, I'm gonna solve 'em
With you riding shotgun, speeding 'cause fuck the cops
